episodes.title: LIVE | Epstein Victim Breaks Down During Hearing, Reveals Details Of His House Arrest | APT
episodes.description:
An alleged survivor of disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein testified before a U.S. congressional committee, revealing disturbing details about abuse she says continued even during his period of house arrest.

The woman, identified as “Roza,” told lawmakers she had been trafficked into Epstein’s network and abused for years. During the emotional hearing, she broke down while describing how her identity was revealed in newly released documents.
Roza said she had previously remained anonymous as a “Jane Doe,” but discovered her name appeared more than 500 times in court files that were made public. She said the exposure has forced her to live in constant fear while the “rich and powerful remain protected by secrecy.”

Epstein died in 2019 while awaiting trial on federal sex trafficking charges, but investigations and public scrutiny surrounding his network and the handling of his prosecution continue years later. The congressional hearing is part of ongoing efforts to examine accountability and transparency related to the case.


#JeffreyEpstein #EpsteinCase #EpsteinVictim #CongressHearing #BreakingNews #USPolitics #JusticeForVictims #WorldNews #Investigations #APT

episodes.title: “We Were Abused As Teens”: Epstein Survivors Give Emotional Testimony During House Hearing | APT
episodes.description:
Multiple survivors of Jeffrey Epstein spoke before a U.S. House hearing, sharing emotional testimony about the abuse they suffered as teenagers and the failures of the justice system.
Jenna Lisa Jones told lawmakers she was only 14 years old when the abuse began in Palm Beach and said it took years for her to find the courage to speak publicly.
Another survivor, Dani Bensky, said she still lives with PTSD after being abused in 2004 and 2005 — years after earlier reports about Epstein had already been made to authorities.
Survivors said a secret non-prosecution deal allowed Epstein to avoid harsher charges, arguing the government failed to inform victims and denied them justice. The testimony came as lawmakers continue to examine the broader network around Epstein and calls for greater accountability.

Epstein survivors testified publicly for the first time before the U.S. House Oversight Committee as Congressman Robert Garcia accused multiple governments and institutions of enabling a decades-long cover-up.
Garcia said survivors were ignored, re-victimized, and even placed in danger while seeking justice in the case involving convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

episodes.description:
Join this channel to get access to perks:

A new installation in Manhattan has transformed millions of pages of documents related to the Epstein case into thousands of bound volumes, creating a large-scale physical archive in New York City.

The New York City reading room, titled the Donald J. Trump and Jeffrey Epstein Memorial Reading Room, contains approximately 3.5 million pages printed and organized into around 3,500 books.

The exhibit allows limited access for journalists, researchers, lawmakers, and victims’ representatives by appointment, while general public access is restricted to viewing the installation. Organizers say the archive is intended to highlight the scale of the released documents and the ongoing discussions around accountability and justice.

episodes.description:
Join this channel to get access to perks:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCpLEtz3H0jSfEneSdf1YKnw/join

A U.S. federal judge has released a document described as a purported suicide note attributed to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, raising renewed attention to the circumstances surrounding his 2019 death.

The handwritten note, reportedly found in a jail cell shared with Nicholas Tartaglione, includes claims about legal investigations and a line referencing “choosing one’s time to say goodbye.” The document was unsealed by the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York following a media request.

Epstein was arrested in 2019 on federal sex trafficking charges and died later that year in a Manhattan jail cell, with his death officially ruled a suicide.

Authorities have not confirmed the authenticity of the newly released document.

episodes.title: Sen. Elizabeth Warren Confronts Fed Chair Nominee Warsh Over Epstein-Linked Assets at Fed Hearing
episodes.description:
During a U.S. Senate Banking Committee hearing in Washington, Elizabeth Warren questioned Federal Reserve chair nominee Kevin Warsh over alleged undisclosed assets and possible financial links to entities associated with Jeffrey Epstein.
Warren pressed Warsh about reports suggesting he holds up to $100 million in assets that had not been fully disclosed, asking whether he had investments in vehicles connected to Epstein.
Warsh responded that the assets in question would be sold if he is confirmed as the next chair of the Federal Reserve. The exchange came as senators scrutinized Warsh’s finances, ethics commitments, and qualifications during the confirmation hearing before the Senate Banking Committee.
The hearing is part of the process to determine whether Warsh will lead the U.S. central bank, one of the most powerful financial institutions in the world.

episodes.title: Starmer Denies Misleading Parliament Over Mandelson Appointment Amid Epstein Row | APT
episodes.description:
Join this channel to get access to perks:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCpLEtz3H0jSfEneSdf1YKnw/join


UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer denied misleading Parliament over the controversial appointment of Peter Mandelson after reporters pressed him during a briefing focused on the Iran crisis.
Journalists questioned Starmer about claims that the appointment process for Mandelson had been “weirdly rushed,” with concerns raised about vetting procedures and Mandelson’s past links to convicted financier Jeffrey Epstein.
Starmer rejected suggestions he misled lawmakers, saying an independent adviser had already reviewed the issue and confirmed that due process had been followed. However, he acknowledged the system itself needed strengthening and said reforms to the vetting process had already begun after earlier revelations about Mandelson’s relationship with Epstein.
The controversy intensified after Mandelson was arrested last month over allegations he passed sensitive information to Epstein during his time as UK business secretary between 2008 and 2010. Mandelson has denied wrongdoing and was later released on bail.
The issue continues to create political pressure for the prime minister as new disclosures — dubbed the “Mandelson files” — raise questions about whether warnings about reputational risks were ignored.

episodes.title: Mandelson Files Reveal Starmer Ignored Epstein Risk Warning | APT
episodes.description:
Newly released “Mandelson files” reveal that UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was warned about potential reputational risks before appointing Peter Mandelson as Britain’s ambassador to the United States. The internal due-diligence memo highlighted Mandelson’s links to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, including alleged contact after Epstein’s 2008 conviction.

Despite the warnings, Mandelson’s appointment was announced in December 2024 and he began his role in Washington in early 2025. Pressure to release the internal documents grew after U.S. Justice Department files suggested Mandelson may have shared sensitive UK government information with Epstein.

British police are now investigating possible misconduct in public office. Mandelson was later dismissed as ambassador in September 2025 after further Epstein files suggested the relationship between the two men was deeper than previously acknowledged. Critics say the documents raise serious questions about judgment inside Downing Street.


#PeterMandelson #KeirStarmer #EpsteinScandal #UKPolitics #DowningStreet #PoliticalScandal #WorldNews #APT

episodes.title: Statue Of Trump And Epstein As ‘Titanic’ Characters Draws Crowds In Washington | APT
episodes.description:
A 12-foot statue depicting US President Donald Trump and convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as the characters Jack and Rose from the film Titanic appeared outside the US Capitol in Washington, drawing crowds of onlookers and photographers.

Footage filmed on Tuesday shows the golden statue surrounded by people taking pictures. The installation portrays Trump standing behind Epstein with their arms extended in the film’s iconic pose.

A plaque titled “The King of the World” references the movie’s storyline while criticizing the relationship between the two men.

The artwork was created by The Secret Handshake, an anonymous activist group responsible for several anti-Trump and anti-Epstein installations previously placed near the Capitol.

According to the group, the statue is expected to remain on display until Friday, March 13.

The installation appeared as questions emerged about the US Justice Department’s handling of records related to Jeffrey Epstein after reports said files linked to allegations involving Trump were missing from a public database.

The Justice Department said the omission was caused by a coding error, not a deliberate removal.

Jeffrey Epstein, a financier convicted of soliciting prostitution involving a minor, died in jail in 2019 while awaiting trial on federal sex-trafficking charges.

Donald Trump has denied any wrongdoing, and officials note that inclusion in the files does not imply criminal conduct.

#DonaldTrump #JeffreyEpstein #USCapitol #BreakingNews #WashingtonDC #PoliticalArt #ProtestArt #TitanicPose #PublicInstallation #USPolitics #JusticeDepartment #EpsteinCase #PoliticalProtest #CapitolHill

episodes.title: Rep. Garcia Dares Trump to “Look Up” at Epstein Survivors in House Gallery at SOTU | APT
episodes.description:
Representative Robert Garcia delivered a sharply worded speech at the “People’s State of the Union,” directly invoking Jeffrey Epstein survivors and daring President Donald Trump to “look up” at the House gallery and face those “demanding justice, truth and accountability.”

Speaking alongside Human Rights Campaign President Kelly Robinson, Garcia centered survivors in his remarks, referencing Annie Farmer and others who endured abuse linked to Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell. He argued that ongoing investigations must prioritize survivors and warned against political deflection surrounding the case.

Garcia also accused the administration and the Department of Justice of shielding powerful figures, calling the situation a “cover up” and stating that the investigation into Epstein’s network is “just getting started.” His remarks came as Trump delivered his State of the Union address, highlighting the political tension between the official speech inside the Capitol and the counter-programmed rally outside.

The speech framed the moment as one of accountability, civil rights advocacy, and survivor-centered justice, with organizers positioning the event as a platform for communities and lawmakers critical of current federal policies.

The “People’s State of the Union,” hosted by MeidasTouch, MoveOn Civic Action, and coalition partners, featured lawmakers, activists, and affected Americans speaking on justice, healthcare, LGBTQ rights, and government accountability during one of the most closely watched political nights in the United States.

#mediastouch #trump #apt

episodes.title: Newly Released Emails Tie Epstein to 'Devil’s Breath' Drug | APT
episodes.description:
Newly released Epstein Files have revealed disturbing new emails suggesting Jeffrey Epstein inquired about “trumpet plants at nursery” — a reference experts say may point to Angel’s Trumpet, a highly toxic plant known to produce scopolamine.

Often called “Devil’s Breath” or the “zombie drug,” scopolamine has been linked to memory loss, impaired decision-making, extreme suggestibility, and in severe cases, paralysis or death. Reports from TMZ, AOL, and others detail emails showing Epstein not only asking about the plants but also receiving articles explaining the drug’s effects.

The latest document dump adds another controversial layer to the ongoing scrutiny of Epstein’s activities. A victim statement included in the files allegedly describes symptoms consistent with scopolamine exposure.

In this video, we break down:
• What Angel’s Trumpet is
• What scopolamine does to the body
• What the newly released emails say
• Why this is raising new questions

Jeffrey Epstein died in 2019 while awaiting trial on federal sex-trafficking charges, but court-ordered document releases continue to generate headlines.

This video discusses allegations and reported document findings. No new criminal charges have been announced in connection to these claims.

#EpsteinFiles #JeffreyEpstein #Scopolamine #AngelTrumpet #BreakingNews #EpsteinUpdate #DevilsBreath #TrueCrime #InvestigativeNews #DocumentDump #GhislaineMaxwell #PoliticalNews
